stories.title: Keir Starmer: I am most proud of lifting two-child benefit cap
stories.summary: Prime Minister Keir Starmer has listed lifting the two-child benefit cap among the highlights of his time in office, as he prepares to leave Downing Street. Starmer took to social media to reflect on his tenure's key achievements.

stories.title: Burnham will change No 10 'boys club' briefing culture, says Lucy Powell
stories.summary: Deputy Labour leader Lucy Powell says No 10 must become more meritocratic as party's female MPs press Andy Burnham on gender balance ahead of his expected arrival as Prime Minister.

stories.title: Can Andy Burnham's devolution promises work in Yorkshire?
stories.summary: Andy Burnham is promising to deliver 'the biggest rebalancing of power our country has ever seen' as he prepares to take over as PM. The BBC examines whether his devolution pledges can work across England.

topics.name: Parliament
topics.description: House of Commons, House of Lords, PMQs & Legislation

stories.title: What's coming up in Parliament this week? 6–9 July 2026
stories.summary: MPs will consider Lords amendments to the National Security (State Threats) Bill this week, while the Conservatives will choose the subject of the second Opposition Day debate. The Treasury Committee will also question Defence Ministers on defence spending.

stories.title: Lords amendments to National Security (State Threats) Bill head to Commons
stories.summary: The House of Commons will review amendments passed by the House of Lords to the National Security (State Threats) Bill, a key piece of national security legislation. This marks a key stage in the bill's parliamentary passage.

topics.name: Labour Party
topics.description: Labour Party news, policy & internal politics

stories.title: The massive scramble behind the scenes before the new PM arrives
stories.summary: As Starmer prepares to leave office, there is a 'bunfight for jobs' in Whitehall with senior figures positioning themselves ahead of Andy Burnham's arrival as Prime Minister, according to BBC political editor Laura Kuenssberg.

stories.title: Labour should win next election under Burnham after work already done, Starmer says
stories.summary: Outgoing PM Keir Starmer has expressed confidence that Labour will win the next general election under Andy Burnham, saying the groundwork has already been laid.

stories.title: Burnham's funding gap: what state are UK finances in for the PM-in-waiting?
stories.summary: Analysis of the financial challenges Andy Burnham will inherit as he prepares to become Prime Minister, with questions raised about the state of public finances.

stories.title: 'No way back,' Kemi Badenoch tells defectors begging to return
stories.summary: Conservative Party leader Kemi Badenoch has told politicians and supporters who abandoned the party for Reform UK that they will not be allowed to return, taking a firm line against defectors.

stories.title: Conservatives to set second Opposition Day debate agenda
stories.summary: With Parliament returning this week, the Conservative Party will choose the subject for the second Opposition Day debate, a key opportunity for the official opposition to set the parliamentary agenda.

stories.title: Lib Dems mark two years since record-breaking 2024 General Election
stories.summary: The Liberal Democrats are marking the second anniversary of their record-breaking 2024 general election result, with MPs highlighting policy wins including progress on LGBT veterans compensation.

stories.title: Farage denies rules broken after reports benefits from ally were not declared
stories.summary: Nigel Farage is facing scrutiny after The Sunday Times reported he received support from George Cottrell, who was convicted of fraud in the US, without declaring it. Farage denies breaking the rules.

stories.title: Revealed: Nigel Farage secretly funded by convicted criminal
stories.summary: The Times investigation reveals the Reform UK leader did not declare benefits including staff, security and housing provided by crypto-gambler George Cottrell, who was convicted of fraud in the United States.

stories.title: Could Farage quit? Questions swirl over Reform UK leader's future
stories.summary: The Guardian reports Farage is under pressure from multiple directions: a £5m undeclared gift, recent byelection losses, and the rise of rival party Restore. Allies dismiss the exit speculation as 'wishful thinking'.

stories.title: Stop the War criticises SNP's 'crazy' response to defence investment plan
stories.summary: The SNP's opposition to Keir Starmer's defence investment plan has drawn criticism from within the peace movement itself, with Stop the War calling the response an 'affront' to party supporters who joined on the back of peace principles.

stories.title: John Swinney lobs Stephen Flynn a food price cap hand grenade
stories.summary: First Minister John Swinney has put Stephen Flynn in a difficult position over food pricing policy, with political tensions rising in Scotland over the SNP's post-election direction.

stories.title: Scottish Conservative MSP demands answers from Swinney over Monklands Hospital risks
stories.summary: A Scottish Conservative MSP is demanding answers from First Minister John Swinney over when risks to the £2.1bn Monklands Hospital replacement were flagged, accusing the SNP government of a cover-up.

stories.title: Burnham rules out early election and announces 'No 10 North' plans
stories.summary: PM-in-waiting Andy Burnham says he has yet to choose his chancellor and wants a 'complete rethink' of UK governance, including his 'No 10 North' proposal to rebalance power from London to the regions.

stories.title: Scramble for Whitehall jobs ahead of PM transition
stories.summary: Senior civil servants and political advisers are jostling for positions as the transition from Starmer to Burnham begins, with one MP describing it as a 'bunfight for jobs' across Whitehall departments.

topics.name: Elections & By-elections
topics.description: Upcoming or recent elections, polls & results

stories.title: Could Farage quit? Reform UK faces pressure after byelection losses
stories.summary: Reform UK's Nigel Farage faces mounting pressure including recent byelection losses alongside the funding scandal, with the Guardian reporting questions about his future as party leader.

stories.title: Why voting method change matters in Greater Manchester mayoral poll
stories.summary: A polling expert says Labour could benefit most from the return of the Supplementary Votes (SV) system in the Greater Manchester mayoral race, which will decide Andy Burnham's successor as mayor.

stories.title: What impact will Andy Burnham have on Greens and Zack Polanski?
stories.summary: The Guardian examines how Burnham's elevation to PM could affect the Green Party's electoral prospects, as voters who left Labour for the Greens under Starmer may now return.

stories.title: Burial plots, lobbying transparency and Nature's rights debated in Lords
stories.summary: The House of Lords held its first round of debates on 3 July covering topics including burial plots, lobbying transparency, and the concept of rights for nature. Healthcare services were also debated in the upper chamber.

stories.title: Lords amendments to National Security (State Threats) Bill head to MPs
stories.summary: The House of Lords has passed amendments to the National Security (State Threats) Bill which will now be considered by the House of Commons this week, marking a key legislative ping-pong moment.
